Name: k1n-baseline-web-acl
Description: k1N baseline WebACL using AWS managed rule groups — review and tune before production use

ExcludedRules.Name: SizeRestrictions_BODY
ExcludedRules.Comment: Excluded to avoid blocking large file uploads — re-enable if not accepting large payloads

ExcludedRules.Name: GenericLFI_BODY
ExcludedRules.Comment: May cause false positives on apps with file path inputs — validate before enabling

OverrideAction.Comment: Use None to enforce the managed rule group actions. Switch to Count to log-only.
SQLi rule set — no exclusions by default, validate with your application
Blocks known bad inputs including Log4Shell patterns and SSRF probes
Blocks IPs on AWS threat intelligence lists — low false positive risk

ExcludedRules.Name: HostingProviderIPList
ExcludedRules.Comment: Excluded — many legitimate SaaS clients and CI/CD agents run in cloud hosting providers

OverrideAction.Comment: Blocks Tor exit nodes and known anonymizing proxies. HostingProviderIPList excluded by default.

_k1n_metadata.deployment_notes: Deploy all rules in Count (override) mode first. Review CloudWatch metrics for AWSWAFv2 blocked requests. Switch to None (enforce) after validating false positive rate.
_k1n_metadata.monitoring_notes: Enable AWS WAF logging to S3 or CloudWatch Logs. Set up CloudWatch alarms on AllowedRequests and BlockedRequests metrics. A spike in blocked requests may indicate active scanning.
_k1n_metadata.tuning_notes: Review excluded rules after initial deployment. Re-enable excluded rules one at a time to validate false positive impact.
